---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

Driscoll et al. () estimate that 53000 km2 of lakes in the US have an advisory warning for mercury, along with 1,500,000 km of rivers.

*What is the total area of lakes in the US?*

Based on a very quick scan of the largest lake areas, I'd guess total lentic area on the order of 500,000km2. 

So to a very rough (and conservative) approximation, 10% of surface freshwater has the potential for mercury bioaccumulation.

Driscoll, C. T., Han, Y. J., Chen, C. Y., Evers, D. C., Lambert, K. F., Holsen, T. M., ... & Munson, R. K. (2007). Mercury contamination in forest and freshwater ecosystems in the northeastern United States. BioScience, 57(1), 17-28.


2025-11-24
- Updated the ACSP_Data to remove 2 duplicates. There are now 309 observations instead of 311
- Refit models
